parts.answer: The only correct answer is A B is incorrect because pathogens are any type of organism that can cause disease C is incorrect because pathogens are any type of organism that can cause disease and can be found in the cells and tissues as well as the blood D is incorrect because pathogens are any type of organism that can cause disease and can be found in the cells and tissues as well as the blood (1) number Additional guidance Mark An answer that includes two of the following points: Any two from: respiratory system / nose / mouth / airways / lungs / inhalation / breathing in urinogenital tract / reproductive system / genitals / sexual transmission damaged skin vector / injections / bites / transfusions (into blood) eyes ears (1) IGNORE digestive system / names parts of digestive system e.g.anus ACCEPT descriptions e.g. open wound IGNORE direct contact unless qualified e.g. passing on athletes foot (1 ) number Additional guidance Mark An answer that includes two of the following points: Any two from: skin / keratin / sebum / epithelial {tissue / cells} skin flora gut flora stomach acid / HCl / hydrochloric acid mucus / cilia blood clots ear wax {antimicrobials / enzymes / lysozyme} in {tears / saliva} eyebrows / eye lashes / nasal hair (1) IGNORE immune system (1 ) number Mark The only correct answer is C A is incorrect because ( 104 ÷ 360) × 100 = 28.888888888888889 = 28.9 B is incorrect because ( 104 ÷ 360) × 100 = 28.888888888888889 = 28.9 D is incorrect because ( 104 ÷ 360) × 100 = 28.888888888888889 = 28.9 (1) number Additional guidance Mark • 37.5 (%) ACCEPT 38 (1) number Additional guidance Mark • phagocytosis of the bacteria (1) • (bacteria destroyed by) {lysosomal / digestive / hydrolytic} enzymes (1) ACCEPT engulf description e.g. surrounded by pseudopodia and taken into the cell IGNORE envelop ACCEPT lysozyme enzymes released from lysosomes digested by enzymes DO NOT ACCEPT lysosome / lysozome (2 ) number Mark

parts.answer: The only correct answer is C A is incorrect because not all cells contain chloroplasts e.g. phloem cells B is incorrect because not all cells contain chloroplasts e.g. phloem cells D is incorrect because not all cells contain chloroplasts e.g. phloem cells (1) number Answer Additional guidance Mark An answer that includes the following points: • drawings : granum / grana / thylakoid AND lamellae (1) IGNORE other chloroplast structures drawn DO NOT ACCEPT non-chloroplast structures e.g. mitochondria, ER • one labelled membrane (1) • second labelled membrane (1) Correct labels: granum / grana (membrane) thylakoid (membrane) (stromal) lamellae / intergranal membrane / intergranal thylakoid IGNORE inner and outer membranes / envelope labelled (3 ) number Mark The only correct answer is A B is incorrect because RUBISCO is found in the stroma C is incorrect because RUBISCO is found in the stroma D is incorrect because RUBISCO is found in the stroma (1) lamellae granum thylakoid number Additional guidance Mark

• 3.48 × 10 / 3.48 · 10 / 3.5 × 10 / 3.5 · 10 (1) (1) number Additional guidance Mark • description of anthropogenic activity ( 1) • explanation of how it affects coral reef (1) (2) number Answer Additional guidance Mark An explanation that includes the following points: • sexual reproduction increases the genetic {variation / diversity} so that {some coral / more coral} is likely to {survive / adapt} (1) • asexual reproduction {retains alleles that are suitable for survival / is faster / only requires one individual coral / produces large numbers of coral} (1) ACCEPT the idea that environmental change, e.g. disease, will not wipe out lots of coral IGNORE easier descriptions of how they reproduce (2) number Answer Additional guidance Mark An answer that includes the following points: • description of difficulty (1) • explanation (1) (2) number Additional guidance Mark

An answer that includes the following points: • (overall) diversity increases in all three types of coral OR the diversity increases (in the first couple of months) and then levels off (in all types / in types P and S) (1) • type A coral supports the highest diversity (1) ACCEPT type S (and P) supports the lowest diversity (2) number Additional guidance Mark

A description that includes two of the following points: • {(counting) the number of / count the} (different) species (algae and animals) (1) • {(counting) the number / determining the abundance / determining the population of} each species (1) • {calculate / find / use} the (bio)diversity index / use the formula 푁푁 ( 푁푁−1 ) 훴훴훴훴 ( 훴훴−1 ) (1) ACCEPT determine species richness ACCEPT determine the heterozygosity index NB if mp 1 not awarded for counting only coral or fish /animal species, penalise once ACCEPT named index e.g. Shannon, Simpson DO NOT ACCEPT Hardy-Weinberg, other wrongly named tests, wrong formula (2) number Mark The only correct answer is C A is incorrect because it should be per square metre not per metre B is incorrect because it should be per square metre not per metre and per year D is incorrect because it should be per year (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is A B is incorrect because GPP is equal to the sum of NPP and R C is incorrect because GPP is equal to the sum of NPP and R D is incorrect because GPP is equal to the sum of NPP and R (1) number Answer Mark The only correct answer is C A is incorrect because (69 565 – 6 590) ÷ 69 565 = 0.905268 × 100 = 90.5 B is incorrect because (69 565 – 6 590) ÷ 69 565 = 0.905268 × 100 = 90.5 D is incorrect because (69 565 – 6 590) ÷ 69 565 = 0.905268 × 100 = 90.5 (1) number Answer Mark *4(b) Descriptions: e.gs. Graph 1 : over the years / between 1990 and 2018 the number of otters increased Graph 2 : over the years erosion increased and decreased Graph 3 : as the number of otters increased, the predicted rate of erosion decreased Table : presence of otters {stopped the increase in the number of burrows/ increased the mass of roots} Links between data supporting success: • decrease in number of burrows as there are fewer crabs as the otters are feeding on them • fewer burrows so the soil is more stable decreasing erosion • more roots to stabilize the soil so less erosion • mass of pickleweed is

The only correct answer is A B is incorrect because sucrose is made from glucose and fructose C is incorrect because monosaccharides are joined by condensation reactions D is incorrect because sucrose is made from glucose and fructose which are joined by condensation reactions (1) number Answer Additional guidance Mark

An answer that includes two of the following points: • (sucrose) lowers the water potential of their (cytoplasm / cell) / makes the ( cytoplasm / cell) the same water potential as the water (1) • so that water does not move out (of the cytoplasm) by osmosis (1) • so the cytoplasm will not {shrink / decrease in volume} (1) ACCEPT {increases solute concentration of / decreases solute potential of / more hypertonic / makes isotonic} cytoplasm ACCEPT prevents plasmolysis (2 ) number Additional guidance Mark

(concentration) increases with (all) salt concentrations (1) • but no data on how much sucrose is released {without salt / with a control (1) • there is {not a positive correlation / no pattern / a
